Title: Chlorophyllase (PsCLH1) and light-harvesting chlorophyll a/b binding protein 1 (PsLhcb1) and PsLhcb5 maintain petal greenness in Paeonia suffruticosa ‘Lv Mu Yin Yu’

Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHLG; PORA; PORB; NYC1; NOL; HCAR; SGRL
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptome analysis; qRT-PCR; overexpression in tobacco; VIGS silencing in P. suffruticosa petals; chlorophyll measurement; color parameter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: initial step of chlorophyll catabolic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in tobacco reduced plant height, leaf number, crown width, fruit number per plant, and single fruit weight
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq 2000); transcriptome data accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydrolyzes chlorophyll to produce chlorophyllide and phytol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCLH1 was identified from transcriptome as the chlorophyll degradation gene with the most significant change during petal degreening from S3 to S5. 2. PsCLH1 overexpression in tobacco caused paler/less green leaves and reduced chlorophyll content. 3. PsCLH1 silencing in 'LMYY' petals increased chlorophyll content and made petals greener, demonstrating that PsCLH1 promotes petal degreening.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest around S3 and significantly changed during S3 to S5 petal degreening
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ‘Lv Mu Yin Yu’; tobacco cv. Nc89
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: decreases chlorophyll accumulation and promotes petal degreening
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although expression peaked with high chlorophyll at S3, discussion notes possible post-translational regulation and latent chlorophyllase function; conflicting CLH necessity is discussed from Arabidopsis literature.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: petals during flower development, especially dynamic from S1 to S5 and critical during S3 to S5
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigment determining petal greenness and flower color

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptome analysis; qRT-PCR; overexpression in tobacco; VIGS silencing in P. suffruticosa petals; chlorophyll measurement; color parameter analysis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for chlorophyll accumulation in petals, not established as rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in tobacco increased plant height, leaf number, crown width, fruit number per plant, and single fruit weight
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq 2000); transcriptome data accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsLhcb1 was strongly downregulated during petal degreening and selected as a key Lhc gene. 2. Overexpression in tobacco increased leaf greenness and chlorophyll levels. 3. Silencing in 'LMYY' petals sharply reduced chlorophyll and accelerated petal degreening, showing PsLhcb1 represses degreening.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high before degreening and significantly downregulated from S3 to S5 in petals
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ‘Lv Mu Yin Yu’; tobacco cv. Nc89
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases chlorophyll accumulation and represses petal degreening
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains many light-responsive elements plus MeJA-, auxin-responsive and MYB-binding elements; identified as nearest to AtLhcb1 by phylogeny.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: petals during flower development, especially high at greener stages and reduced during blooming-associated degreening
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigment determining petal greenness and flower color

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptome analysis; qRT-PCR; overexpression in tobacco; VIGS silencing in P. suffruticosa petals; chlorophyll measurement; color parameter analysis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: photosystem II antenna/light-harvesting complex; CP26-related subgroup assignment discussed
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for chlorophyll accumulation in petals, not established as rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in tobacco increased plant height, leaf number, crown width, fruit number per plant, and single fruit weight
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq 2000); transcriptome data accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsLhcb5 was strongly downregulated during petal degreening and selected as a key Lhc gene. 2. Overexpression in tobacco increased leaf greenness and chlorophyll levels. 3. Silencing in 'LMYY' petals sharply reduced chlorophyll and accelerated petal degreening, showing PsLhcb5 represses degreening.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high before degreening and significantly downregulated from S3 to S5 in petals
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ‘Lv Mu Yin Yu’; tobacco cv. Nc89
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases chlorophyll accumulation and represses petal degreening
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named by phylogeny as nearest to AtLhcb5, although text also notes CP26 subfamily relationship for Pos.gene23725; promoter contains many light-responsive elements plus MeJA-, auxin-responsive and MYB-binding elements.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: petals during flower development, especially high at greener stages and reduced during blooming-associated degreening
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigment determining petal greenness and flower color
